rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WESTERLIND VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Commence Wrongful Death Action Pursuant To C.C.P. Section §340.2 And Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To C.C.P. §§583.410-583.420
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Commence Wrongful Death Action Pursuant To C.C.P. Section §340.2 And Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To C.C.P. §§583.410-583.420. To be heard on November 16, 2009, per amended Notice filed on October 20, 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained. Plaintiff fails to state facts regarding exposure to defendant's alleged injury-producing toxin, or to sufficiently identify the injury-producing toxin. (See Bockrath vs. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71.) Insufficient facts alleged regarding third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Hearing required to determine whether leave to amend will be granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT JANUS CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, no opposition filed. Plaintiff to plead facts with particularity regarding extreme/outrageous conduct. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HANMI BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. PLEASANTON SUSHI & SEAFOOD BUFFET, INC., A et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF HANMI BANK, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. RICHARD BOGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to first cause of action for breach of contract. Objections to interrogatories not evidence that cross-complainant cannot prove his case. First Amended cross-complaint sufficient to maintain breach of contract cause of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend second cause of action for fraud to plead specific facts regarding the misrepresentations, what they were, when, where, and by what means made (written or oral?). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NEWPORT BUILDERS INC, A NEVADA CORPORATION et al VS. NORTH BAY CONSTRUCTION, INC, A CALIFORNIA et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFFS NEWPORT BUILDERS INC, AND, NEWPORT BUILDERS, L.P., Motion for and Order Allowing the Filing of a Second Amended Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL KINGDOM USA INC. VS. 3T HERBTECH INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Jing Tang Liu
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT 3T HERBTECH INC. Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Jing Tang Liu. Motion granted. Plaintiff fails to meet its evidentiary burden by demonstrating that the San Gabriel address is Liu's "usual place of business" or "usual mailing address." =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WAI MAN CHAN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Review Decision Of Unemployment Insuarance Appeals Board
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 9, 2009, line 12, PETITIONER WAI CHAN Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Review Decision Of Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board. Continued to December 9, 2009. Court strikes Plaintiff Exhibits 15a through 16f because they are not properly part of the administrative record. Respondent and real party in interest to file oppositions to the merits by November 30, 2009. Plaintiff's reply, points and authorities due by December 4, 2009. All parties to personally serve briefs and provide courtesy copies. =(302/CWW)
